definition: Government by the people, often through elected representatives.
The power to act, speak, or think without unjust restraint.
Fair and equal treatment under the law.
Freedoms and protections every person is entitled to.
The act of choosing leaders or policies in an election.
A legally recognized member of a country.
An organized process for choosing public officials by vote.
Treating all people the same in status, rights, and opportunity.
The state of being free within society.
Freedom from disturbance, war, or violence.
A place where legal cases are heard and decided.
What is real and accurate; the foundation of informed citizenship.
The expression of opinion in civic life.
Acting with integrity, respect, and ethical character.
A responsibility a citizen owes to country and community.
A course of action set by a government or organization.
